key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: [Assumption] Mid-cycle normalized owner cash: ~$7.8M annual (2021–2022 operating profit ~$13M less maintenance capital spending ~$6M) scaled to 65.4M diluted shares; current filing OCF minus capital spending is negative ($-0.29/sh FY2025)
Mid-cycle owner cash $0.12/sh; InP AI/datacenter demand via Coherent/Casela contracts; utilization normalizes toward 2021–2022 economics on higher revenue base

open_questions: Confirm mid-cycle owner cash normalization ($0.12/sh) vs spot losses before stance promotion
Tongmei PE redemption negotiation outcome and HK listing timeline
Management alignment on further dilution vs profitability path
Full-tier filing text extract gap: evidence build returns `no_full_tier_text_extract`; capacity/utilization method inputs remain open in `evidence_task_queue.json`
thesis: AXT Inc. (NASDAQ: AXTI) manufactures specialty compound semiconductor wafer substrates and related raw materials. **[Fact]** The company produces indium phosphide (InP), gallium arsenide (GaAs), and germanium (Ge) wafers used when silicon cannot meet frequency or optical performance requirements. **[Fact]** Two consolidated subsidiaries also produce gallium raw materials (85.5% owned). **[Fact]** Manufacturing is concentrated in China through subsidiaries and joint ventures, with corporate headquarters in Fremont, California. **[Fact]** FY2025 revenue was **$88.3 million**; the company reported a **$21.3 million** net loss and **negative** operating cash flow. **[Inference]** Cash to owners today is not positive; the investment case rests on substrate cycle recovery, AI-driven InP demand, and optional value from subsidiary Tongmei's capital-markets path.
why_market_wrong: The market may be pricing **AI optical connectivity optionality** (InP for high-speed data transmission) while underweighting **China manufacturing and export-permit risk**, **customer concentration** (top five customers were **67%** of FY2025 revenue), **share dilution** (shares outstanding rose from roughly **43.5 million** to **65.4 million** between Q1 2025 and May 2026), and **Tongmei private-equity redemption overhang** after the July 2026 withdrawal of the STAR Market listing application. **[Inference]** Q1 2026 revenue of **$26.9 million** (+39% year over year) and gross margin near **30%** suggest operating inflection, but reported owner cash is still negative and mid-cycle normalization requires evidence that new supply contracts convert to sustained free cash flow, not just capacity spending at Tongmei.
